expectations.text: States that bench press is NOT the best measure of overall raw/physical strength
expectations.evidence: Opening states: 'No. The bench press is a sport-specific measure of upper-body pressing strength, not a comprehensive test of raw physical strength.'

expectations.text: Mentions odd-object lifting or strongman-style activities as a better test
expectations.evidence: Does not mention odd-object lifting, Husafell stone, or strongman activities. Instead recommends deadlift and multi-lift totals.

expectations.text: Explains that odd objects are harder to game with technique compared to standardized barbell lifts
expectations.evidence: No mention of odd objects or the technique-gaming argument. Does discuss technique dependency of bench press but in the context of barbell lifts.

expectations.text: Acknowledges bench press is useful for upper-body strength tracking specifically
expectations.evidence: States: 'The bench press is excellent for: Measuring upper-body horizontal pressing strength in a standardized, repeatable way.'
